differential_diagnoses: Drug reaction with eosinophilia and systemic symptoms (DRESS)
next_steps: Isolate the patient with airborne and contact precautions pending definitive diagnosis
Perform PCR testing for varicella-zoster virus and herpes simplex virus from a vesicle/ulcer swab
Perform a punch biopsy of an active lesion for histopathology and immunohistochemical staining
Initiate empiric intravenous acyclovir pending PCR results due to the presence of fever and systemic symptoms
Screen for underlying immunocompromise including HIV testing
Obtain dermatology consultation for clinical evaluation and confirmation of suspected infestation or viral exanthem

differential_diagnoses: Drug reaction with eosinophilia and systemic symptoms (DRESS)
next_steps: Implement strict airborne and contact isolation precautions
Swab skin vesicles and oral ulcer for VZV and HSV PCR
Perform punch biopsy of a representative skin lesion for histopathology and immunohistochemical staining
Initiate empiric intravenous acyclovir pending viral studies
Perform syphilis screening (RPR/VDRL and treponemal-specific test)
Obtain HIV testing and CD4 count
Review peripheral blood smear to characterize atypical lymphocytes
Consult dermatology for formal assessment of rash morphology
